name: corrected-wide-v3-proof-holdout-v1

selection.algorithm: exclude every representative content hash, group remaining rows by family and expected verdict, select the lexicographically smallest content hashes up to each fixed quota, then globally sort by content hash
selection.rare_strata_note: The excluded representative already contains every full-corpus mixed row, the only trivial row, and every comparison/unsat row; the holdout adds all six remaining comparison/sat rows. The combined evidence union therefore retains complete rare-stratum coverage.

execution.source_policy: run from the clean detached Axeyum commit containing this registration

acceptance.not_certified_policy: retain and report every row; cooperative expiry and hard timeout are coverage misses, not solver verdicts or omitted denominator rows
acceptance.repetition_gate: exact source/config/environment/manifest identity and exact per-query outcome, proof, and end-to-end status across both runs

claim_limits: This is a real-query correctness and deployability denominator, not a solver-performance experiment.
The holdout is hash-stratified and verdict-balanced rather than prevalence-weighted; do not extrapolate its percentages to the full corpus.
CNF DRAT and end-to-end faithfulness remain separate assurance statements.
A not-certified row is retained coverage under the fixed resource policy, not a proof failure.
No result from this holdout changes concretization policy, finding recall, or the symbolic-memory gate.
